edges.description: p53 degradation abolishes G1/S and G2/M checkpoints
E2F release activates genes required for DNA replication

nodes.description: HPV E6 oncoprotein recruits the E3 ubiquitin ligase E6AP to target p53 for proteasomal degradation, eliminating DNA damage checkpoints and allowing survival of cells with genomic instability. Unlike HPV-negative HNSCC, TP53 mutations are rare in HPV-positive tumors.

HPV E7 oncoprotein binds and degrades the retinoblastoma protein (pRB), releasing E2F transcription factors to drive S-phase entry. This causes compensatory overexpression of p16INK4a, which serves as a clinical biomarker for HPV-associated tumors.

biological_processes: G1/S transition of mitotic cell cycle
